figures.caption: FIG. 1: (a) Sketch of the problem [adapted from 5]. (b) Schematic of the entropy wave ê(ξ), solid/dashed lines indicate the real/imaginary parts, respectively.

FIG. 2: Acoustic □i (black) and entropic □ii (blue) components with (solid) and without (dashed) the base flow pressure gradient, for a cylinder at M∞ = 7 and freestream entropy wave with ω = 40 (ω̃ = 2.9). (a) Pressure, (b) Velocity, (c) Density.

FIG. 3: (a) Disturbance pressure amplitude on the body, |p̂b|, and (b) Shock velocity amplitude, |iω̃x̂s|, vs. rescaled frequency ω̃, for incoming slow acoustic (solid); entropy (dashed-dotted); and fast acoustic (dashed) waves, for a cylinder at M∞ = 7.

FIG. 4: Maximum amplitude of the disturbance pressure on the body, |p̂b|max, vs. Mach number. Actual flow (blue), uniform flow (black) and no reflections (red). Incoming slow acoustic (solid); entropy (dashed-dotted); and fast acoustic (dashed) waves.
FIG. 5: Corrected figure 2 from Morkovin [5], with +Π31 and −P23.
FIG. 7: Corrected figure 5 from Morkovin [5], | P23 Π21 | (13b) (blue); | P23 Π31 | (13c) (magenta); max of (14) |Π31
TABLE I: Coefficients Λij and Πij.
